checkpoint_context.active_numbering_note: The active checkpoint path advances SANDBOXING_AND_ISOLATION_MODEL-0 as Φ-B2 after replay and snapshot doctrine even where older planning text may still drift.

isolation_definition.sandboxing_summary: Sandboxing is a constrained operational posture inside an explicit isolation boundary that narrows what a subject, interface, or execution context may observe, mutate, invoke, emit, import, or claim.
isolation_definition.isolation_summary: Isolation is the law-governed separation of authority posture, state posture, execution posture, visibility posture, tool posture, or continuity posture between runtime subjects or surfaces.
why_needed: Bounded execution and bounded exposure require lawful separation rather than convenience labels.
Isolation preserves ownership, bridge legality, replay integrity, snapshot legality, and state-class discipline.
Later multi-version, hotswap, distributed-authority, operator, and live-ops work must inherit explicit containment law rather than inventing it ad hoc.

isolation_subject_classes.description: Execution-hosting or execution-coordinating structures whose runtime locality does not imply unrestricted access or mutation.
Mediation, routing, replay-support, snapshot-support, or compatibility services whose reach must remain subordinate to ownership and bridge law.
Grouped bounded components whose shared hosting could otherwise hide coupling, truth ownership, or bridge bypass.
Runtime structures supporting observation, validation, persistence support, or replay support without becoming semantic owners.
Control-plane, negotiation, routing, policy, and integration surfaces close to authority envelopes without being semantic authority.
Read-only adapters, render pipelines, inspection tools, and presentation surfaces that must remain downstream of truth.
CLI, MCP, automation, and operator-mediated paths whose reachability does not imply permission or boundary bypass.
Playtest, research, diagnostic, or provisional structures that must remain explicitly quarantined from default canonical authority.
Future module or packaging realizations that may embody isolated subjects without defining constitutional boundary law by layout alone.

isolation_boundary_classes.description: Preserves semantic ownership and authority posture so shared hosting, convenience roots, or tool reachability do not become ownership reassignment.
Preserves authoritative, derived, transient, projected, and presentation state distinctions across boundaries.
Constrains what execution contexts may run, mutate, schedule, or import so runtime adjacency does not create uncontrolled side effects.
Preserves explicit separation for cross-domain or cross-shard effects whose legality depends on bridge law.
Preserves lawful reenactment, lawful continuity capture, and lawful replay or snapshot interpretation without creating mutation authority.
Preserves the distinction between visibility and authority so render, UI, observability, and inspection remain downstream of truth.
Constrains control, CLI, automation, MCP, and operator surfaces so exposure does not become hidden execution authority.
Keeps experimental, diagnostic, or playtest work explicitly quarantined so provisional behavior does not silently become default authority.

distinction_notes.isolation_boundary: A declared separation of authority, state, execution, visibility, tool, or continuity posture.
distinction_notes.authority_ownership_boundary: A narrower boundary preserving semantic ownership and canonical-root discipline.
distinction_notes.lifecycle_state: An operational posture that may interact with isolation without defining containment by itself.
distinction_notes.replay_snapshot_constraints: Upstream continuity rules that isolation must preserve rather than override.
distinction_notes.task_or_mcp_surface: An exposed interface envelope that does not by itself define isolation law or permission.
distinction_notes.product_session_boundary: A UX shell boundary that is not sufficient as runtime isolation law.
distinction_notes.test_environment: A development or validation context that may exist without lawful authority, state, or bridge separation.

invalidity_degradation_categories.description: The declared isolation class, scope, and constraints are satisfied for the intended bounded purpose.
The boundary exists but some declared separation remains incomplete and must stay explicit.
The boundary remains partially lawful under reduced guarantees, visibility, or allowed actions.
The boundary claims a posture it does not actually satisfy because configuration or wiring is unsound.
The boundary cannot lawfully expose or mutate the requested surfaces because authority posture is insufficient.
The boundary conflicts with ownership law or unresolved ownership review.
The boundary erases or violates required cross-domain bridge mediation.
The boundary traps authoritative or continuity-relevant state in an illicit local form.
The boundary makes lawful replay or lawful snapshot interpretation unavailable without declared basis.
Projected, generated, or presentation state escapes its lawful containment and is treated as canonical.
The boundary confuses read visibility or exposure with authority to mutate or define truth.
The boundary may exist as evidence or a low-risk enclosure but is not fit for privileged, bridge-sensitive, or continuity-sensitive work.
